content_en:
They came out of the capital, Madyan, and got on to their camels with the basket of soil. On reaching the presence of Sa’d bin Abu Waqs +3, they gave him the good news of victory and said:

“Allah Almighty will conquer for the Muslims the land of Persia. And they will be masters of Iran.”

A. few days later the Battle of Qadissiyah was fought and the trenches of the battlefield of Qadissiyah were filled with dead bodies. But all these bodies were those of the Iranian soldiers.

Though the Iranians faced a humiliating defeat at Qadissiyah, they did not lose courage; on the other hand they conscripted new recruits and organised an army numbering one hundred and fifty thousand soldiers. When the Caliph, ‘Umar Farooq %, heard of this huge army, he himself got ready to face this great danger to the Muslim Ummah. But some important personages of the Ummah prevented him from doing so. They suggested that a commander should be given the responsibility of leading the army on this expedition, and it would be better if he continued to remain in the capital, Al-Madinah.

‘Umar Farooq # asked them to suggest the name of one particular person who would best be able to fulfil the responsibility of leading the Muslim army.

They all replied in chorus that he himself knew the army very well. After considering the matter for a few minutes he said:

“swear by Allah Almighty that J will appoint such a person to lead the Muslim Mujahideen who at the time of action in the battlefield will appear swifter than an arrow; sucha person is Nu’man bin Magqran 2. ”

As soon as they heard this they all exclaimed that in truth he was the ideal person for the position of commander. After gaining the vote of confidence of the members of the Advisory Council and the body of Ministers of Administration and Management, ‘Umar Farooq -# wrote the following letter to Nu’man bin Maqran .

“To Nu’man bin Magqran -# from ‘Umar bin Khattab (<4). Assalamu ‘Alikum twa Rahmathullah, my best wishes to you.

I have received news that the Iranians have gathered a huge

armed force in the city of Nahawind. After you receive my letter, please set out to oppose this Iranian army with the Name of Allah Almighty on your lips and with trust in His Assistance. Please remember that a single Muslim is more precious to me than a hundred thousand dinars. With Salam.”

Nu’man bin Magqran .# prepared his army for combat and sent experience warriors to get information about the prevailing situation. As these riders approached the city of Nahawind, their steeds suddenly stopped. The ace riders tried to spur them on but instead of moving forwards they started to rear up. When the riders got off to check on the problem they were surprised to find that nails had pierced their hooves. They immediately informed their Commander, Nu’man bin Maqran of the situation and asked for his opinion or advice on what to do next.

He asked them to stay put as they were and when the darkness of night fell they should light huge bonfires to enable the enemy to spot them in the bright light produced by them. When the light of the fire spread far and wide, they should pretend to be scared and terrified and swiftly race backwards. The enemy should feel that they were terrified and were retreating. Then the enemy would experience an intense desire to capture them; in order to accomplish this they would chase them and themselves get pierced by the nails and thorns they had spread out for the Mujahideen. Thus when these nails and thorns got embedded in the hooves of their steeds, the way would be cleared for the Muslim soldiers. This ploy worked against the Iranians and when in the glow of the fire they saw that the Muslim soldiers wanted to retreat, they sent their workers to clear the roads. When the roads were cleared the Mujahideen suddenly turned around and advanced towards Nahawind and attacked the enemy.

Nu’man bin Magran %, leading his men, reached the northern borders of Nahawind and addressing Mujahideen, said:

“1 will raise my voice in praising Allah Almighty and say ‘Allaho Akbar’. When I raise my voice the first time, you should all become alert, when | call out the second time get your weapons ready. And when I raise my voice the third time, all of you should attack the enemy with me.”

So, Nu’man bin Maqran .% repeated the phrase three times and exhibiting great daring and courage, rushed into the ranks of the enemy. And under his leadership the Mujahideen, swept the enemy like pieces of straw on a forceful current and moved forward rapidly and relentlessly. A forceful battle ensued, such as has been rarely paralleled in history. The Iranian soldiers were mowed down like a sheaves of grain, and one could only see heaps of bodies tying all over the battlefield. The blood of the sinners was flowing like water and the battlefield had become like a swamp. The horse of the Commander-inChief, Nu‘man bin Magran 4, had become uncontrollable and slipped in the slimy mud. As soon as Nu‘man bin Maqran -# fell from his horse he was martyred. His brother stepped forward, picked up the standard, and threw his sheet over his brother's body, so that the rest of the men did not realise their Commander-in-Chief had been martyred.

“Look, this is your General in the eternal sleep of death; having achieved martyrdom he has entered his name in letters of gold among the people in Paradise.”

The eyes of all the loyal Mujahideen were filled with tears at the sight.

“Come back to your Lord, well-pleased (yourself) and wellpleasing (unto Him)!” (89:28)

Qa‘qa‘ bin ‘Amr At-Tameemi 2

On one occasion, the Prophet 3, asked Qa‘qa‘ bin ‘Amr At-Tameemi #, “What preparations have you made for Jihad?” He answered on hearing this:

“I have love and obedience for the Prophet 3B

and a swift and daring horse. This is what I have made ready.”

Qa‘qa‘ bin ‘Amr At-Tameemi

Qa‘qa’ bin ‘Amr At-Tameemi < was a great General of the Muslim army. He was a picture of disciplined obedience, a well built, handsome and attractive great hero of the Muslim Ummah who was extremely dignified. A brave, and daring soldier, an ace rider - Mujahid. Who was faster than wind, and an exemplary champion and supporter of Islam. He put to good use his experience as a warrior and could be depended upon at the most of crucial times. He did not get many opportunities to display his incomparable qualities of valour and military skills during the time of the Prophet 38, because he joined the circle of the followers of Islam late. Nonetheless, in Qadissiyah, Nahawind and in each of the battles against the apostates, he left lasting impressions of martial expertise and skill which are inscribed in history in golden letters. Abu Bakr Siddiq 4 knew very well his incomparable courage, his lightening speed, his foresight and expertise in assaulting the enemy and paid him the compliment quoted above.

After the Battle of Tabook, in the year 9 Hijrah many tribes entered the fold of Islam. In order to receive in person blessings from the benefactor of Mankind, the Prophet Muhammad 38, many crowds came to delight in his company and see him with their own eyes.

very angry and addressing another trusted Companion, Prophet 3 asked:

“Who will go to accomplish this mission?” ‘Ainiah bin Badr said:

“O Prophet 38 may my mother and my father be sacrificed for you, please give me a chance to serve you. | would love to volunteer myself.”

The benefactor of Mankind, the Prophet 3%, appointed him an officer and placed fifty Mujahideen under him. He attacked the Banu Tameem, defeated them and captured eleven men and women and brought them back to Al-Madinah. Among the prisoners was the Chieftain, ‘Atarad. When these prisoners were brought in, the Prophet % happened to be ina private room next to his mosque. Bilal 4 had given the Adhan for the Dhuhr prayer and the Prophet 3 was a little late in coming out to lead the prayers. The prisoners who were in chains outside the Prophet’s mosque became restless. One blunt outspoken prisoner, Iqr’a bin Habis, called out loudly from outside the room of the Prophet #8:

“Mister, release me from this imprisonment! You should be aware of the fact that I am a famous poet of my tribe. When I write verses praising anyone, the whole world looks at him with envy. And when I decide to become critical of any person, then I sow such seeds of hatred and scorn that the man is not even able to lift up his head and walk upright among his peers. It is better for you O Prophet 3% that you release a man like me who does not care for the world or its material pleasures and attractions. Or else the outcome of your actions will not be good.”

The benefactor of Mankind, the Prophet 38 replied in gentle tones:

“You are speaking lie. Respect or disrespect in the eyes of the world is in the total control of Allah 38.”

After answering to the man’s insolent and rude challenge calmly and softly, he walked into the mosque and led the prayers with full concentration and devotion. Having offered his prayers to his Creature, the Prophet 38 turned his attention towards the prisoners,
content_id:
Mereka keluar dari ibu kota, Madyan, dan menaiki unta-unta mereka dengan membawa keranjang berisi tanah. Setibanya di hadapan Sa'd bin Abu Waqqas رضي الله عنه, mereka menyampaikan kabar gembira tentang kemenangan dan berkata:

"Allah Subhanahu wa Ta'ala akan menaklukkan bagi kaum muslimin tanah Persia. Dan mereka akan menjadi penguasa Iran."

Beberapa hari kemudian Pertempuran Qadisiyyah berkecamuk dan parit-parit medan perang Qadisiyyah dipenuhi oleh jasad-jasad. Namun semua jasad itu adalah milik para prajurit Iran.

Meskipun pasukan Iran mengalami kekalahan yang memalukan di Qadisiyyah, mereka tidak kehilangan keberanian; sebaliknya mereka merekrut tentara baru dan mengorganisir pasukan yang berjumlah seratus lima puluh ribu prajurit. Ketika Khalifah 'Umar Farooq رضي الله عنه mendengar tentang pasukan besar ini, beliau sendiri bersiap untuk menghadapi bahaya besar bagi ummah Muslim ini. Namun beberapa tokoh penting dari ummah mencegahnya melakukan hal tersebut. Mereka menyarankan agar seorang panglima diberikan tanggung jawab memimpin pasukan dalam ekspedisi ini, dan akan lebih baik jika beliau tetap berada di ibu kota, Al-Madinah.

'Umar Farooq رضي الله عنه meminta mereka untuk mengusulkan nama satu orang tertentu yang paling mampu memenuhi tanggung jawab memimpin pasukan Muslim.

Mereka semua menjawab serempak bahwa beliau sendiri yang paling mengenal pasukan dengan baik. Setelah mempertimbangkan masalah tersebut selama beberapa menit, beliau berkata:

"Aku bersumpah demi Allah Subhanahu wa Ta'ala bahwa aku akan mengangkat seseorang untuk memimpin para mujahidin Muslim yang pada saat beraksi di medan perang akan tampak lebih cepat dari anak panah; orang tersebut adalah Nu'man bin Muqarrin رضي الله عنه."

Begitu mereka mendengar hal ini, mereka semua berseru bahwa sesungguhnya beliaulah orang yang paling ideal untuk posisi panglima. Setelah mendapatkan mosi kepercayaan dari para anggota Dewan Penasehat dan badan Menteri Administrasi dan Manajemen, 'Umar Farooq رضي الله عنه menulis surat berikut kepada Nu'man bin Muqarrin رضي الله عنه.

"Kepada Nu'man bin Muqarrin رضي الله عنه dari 'Umar bin Khattab رضي الله عنه. Assalamu 'Alaikum wa Rahmatullah, salam terbaik untukmu.

Aku telah menerima kabar bahwa pasukan Iran telah mengumpulkan kekuatan

bersenjata yang besar di kota Nahawand. Setelah engkau menerima suratku, berangkatlah untuk menghadapi pasukan Iran ini dengan Nama Allah Subhanahu wa Ta'ala di bibirmu dan dengan kepercayaan pada Pertolongan-Nya. Ingatlah bahwa seorang Muslim lebih berharga bagiku daripada seratus ribu dinar. Wassalam."

Nu'man bin Muqarrin رضي الله عنه menyiapkan pasukannya untuk pertempuran dan mengirim para pejuang berpengalaman untuk mendapatkan informasi tentang situasi yang sedang berlangsung. Ketika para penunggang kuda ini mendekati kota Nahawand, kuda-kuda mereka tiba-tiba berhenti. Para penunggang ulung itu mencoba memacu mereka, tetapi alih-alih bergerak maju, kuda-kuda itu malah meringkik dan berdiri dengan kaki belakang. Ketika para penunggang turun untuk memeriksa masalahnya, mereka terkejut menemukan bahwa paku-paku telah menusuk kuku kuda mereka. Mereka segera memberitahu Panglima mereka, Nu'man bin Muqarrin رضي الله عنه tentang situasi tersebut dan meminta pendapat atau nasihatnya tentang apa yang harus dilakukan selanjutnya.

Beliau meminta mereka untuk tetap di tempat dan ketika kegelapan malam tiba, mereka harus menyalakan api unggun besar agar musuh dapat melihat mereka dalam cahaya terang yang dihasilkannya. Ketika cahaya api menyebar jauh dan luas, mereka harus berpura-pura ketakutan dan ngeri lalu bergegas mundur dengan cepat. Musuh harus merasa bahwa mereka ketakutan dan mundur. Maka musuh akan merasakan keinginan kuat untuk menangkap mereka; untuk mencapai ini mereka akan mengejar dan mereka sendiri akan tertusuk oleh paku-paku dan duri-duri yang telah mereka sebarkan untuk para mujahidin. Dengan demikian ketika paku-paku dan duri-duri ini tertanam di kuku kuda mereka, jalan akan terbuka bagi para prajurit Muslim. Siasat ini berhasil melawan pasukan Iran dan ketika dalam cahaya api mereka melihat bahwa para prajurit Muslim hendak mundur, mereka mengirim para pekerja untuk membersihkan jalan. Ketika jalan sudah bersih, para mujahidin tiba-tiba berbalik dan maju menuju Nahawand dan menyerang musuh.

Nu'man bin Muqarrin رضي الله عنه, memimpin pasukannya, mencapai perbatasan utara Nahawand dan menyeru para mujahidin, berkata:

"Aku akan mengangkat suaraku memuji Allah Subhanahu wa Ta'ala dan mengucapkan 'Allahu Akbar'. Ketika aku mengangkat suaraku pertama kali, kalian semua harus waspada, ketika aku berseru kedua kalinya siapkan senjata kalian. Dan ketika aku mengangkat suaraku ketiga kalinya, kalian semua harus menyerang musuh bersamaku."

Maka, Nu'man bin Muqarrin رضي الله عنه mengulang kalimat itu tiga kali dan menunjukkan keberanian dan ketangguhan yang luar biasa, menyerbu ke barisan musuh. Dan di bawah kepemimpinannya para mujahidin menyapu musuh seperti jerami di atas arus yang deras dan bergerak maju dengan cepat dan tanpa henti. Pertempuran dahsyat pun terjadi, yang jarang ada bandingannya dalam sejarah. Para prajurit Iran ditebas bagaikan berkas-berkas gandum, dan yang terlihat hanyalah tumpukan jasad bergelimpangan di seluruh medan perang. Darah orang-orang berdosa mengalir seperti air dan medan perang telah menjadi seperti rawa. Kuda Panglima Tertinggi, Nu'man bin Muqarrin رضي الله عنه, menjadi tidak terkendali dan tergelincir di lumpur yang licin. Begitu Nu'man bin Muqarrin رضي الله عنه terjatuh dari kudanya, beliau gugur sebagai syahid. Saudaranya melangkah maju, mengambil panji, dan melemparkan kainnya menutupi jasad saudaranya, agar pasukan lainnya tidak menyadari bahwa Panglima Tertinggi mereka telah gugur syahid.

"Lihatlah, inilah Jenderal kalian dalam tidur abadi kematian; setelah meraih kesyahidan, beliau telah mengukir namanya dengan huruf emas di antara penghuni Surga."

Mata seluruh mujahidin yang setia dipenuhi air mata melihat pemandangan itu.

"Kembalilah kepada Tuhanmu dengan hati yang ridha dan diridhai!" (QS. Al-Fajr: 28)

Qa'qa' bin 'Amr At-Tameemi رضي الله عنه

Pada suatu kesempatan, Nabi ﷺ bertanya kepada Qa'qa' bin 'Amr At-Tameemi رضي الله عنه, "Persiapan apa yang telah engkau buat untuk jihad?" Beliau menjawab setelah mendengar ini:

"Aku memiliki cinta dan ketaatan kepada Nabi ﷺ

dan seekor kuda yang cepat dan berani. Inilah yang telah aku siapkan."

Qa'qa' bin 'Amr At-Tameemi

Qa'qa' bin 'Amr At-Tameemi رضي الله عنه adalah seorang Jenderal besar pasukan Muslim. Beliau adalah gambaran ketaatan yang disiplin, seorang pahlawan besar ummah Muslim yang bertubuh tegap, tampan dan menarik serta sangat berwibawa. Seorang prajurit yang pemberani dan tangguh, penunggang kuda ulung - mujahid. Yang lebih cepat dari angin, dan seorang pejuang teladan serta pembela Islam. Beliau memanfaatkan dengan baik pengalamannya sebagai pejuang dan dapat diandalkan pada saat-saat paling krusial. Beliau tidak mendapat banyak kesempatan untuk menunjukkan kualitas keberanian dan kemampuan militernya yang tiada tanding selama masa Nabi ﷺ, karena beliau bergabung dalam lingkaran pengikut Islam terlambat. Meskipun demikian, di Qadisiyyah, Nahawand dan dalam setiap pertempuran melawan kaum murtad, beliau meninggalkan kesan keahlian dan keterampilan perang yang abadi yang terukir dalam sejarah dengan huruf emas. Abu Bakr Siddiq رضي الله عنه sangat mengetahui keberanian beliau yang tiada tanding, kecepatan kilat beliau, pandangan jauh ke depan dan keahliannya dalam menyerang musuh dan memberikan pujian yang dikutip di atas.

Setelah Pertempuran Tabuk, pada tahun 9 Hijriah banyak suku memeluk Islam. Dalam rangka menerima secara langsung berkah dari sang dermawan bagi Umat Manusia, Nabi Muhammad ﷺ, banyak rombongan datang untuk berbahagia menemani beliau dan melihat beliau dengan mata kepala sendiri.

sangat marah dan menyeru sahabat terpercaya lainnya, Nabi ﷺ bertanya:

"Siapa yang akan pergi menyelesaikan misi ini?" 'Uyainah bin Badr berkata:

"Wahai Nabi ﷺ semoga ibu dan ayahku dikorbankan untukmu, berikanlah aku kesempatan untuk melayanimu. Aku ingin sekali menawarkan diriku."

Sang dermawan bagi Umat Manusia, Nabi ﷺ, mengangkatnya sebagai perwira dan menempatkan lima puluh mujahidin di bawah pimpinannya. Beliau menyerang Banu Tameem, mengalahkan mereka dan menawan sebelas pria dan wanita lalu membawa mereka kembali ke Al-Madinah. Di antara para tawanan terdapat sang Kepala Suku, 'Atarad. Ketika para tawanan ini dibawa masuk, Nabi ﷺ kebetulan berada di ruangan pribadi di samping masjidnya. Bilal رضي الله عنه telah mengumandangkan adzan untuk salat Dzuhur dan Nabi ﷺ sedikit terlambat keluar untuk memimpin salat. Para tawanan yang dirantai di luar masjid Nabi menjadi gelisah. Seorang tawanan yang blak-blakan, Iqra' bin Habis, berteriak keras dari luar kamar Nabi ﷺ:

"Tuan, bebaskan aku dari penjara ini! Engkau harus tahu bahwa aku adalah penyair terkenal dari sukuku. Ketika aku menulis syair memuji seseorang, seluruh dunia memandangnya dengan iri. Dan ketika aku memutuskan untuk mengkritik seseorang, maka aku menaburkan benih kebencian dan cemoohan sedemikian rupa sehingga orang itu bahkan tidak mampu mengangkat kepalanya dan berjalan tegak di antara rekan-rekannya. Lebih baik bagimu wahai Nabi ﷺ membebaskan orang sepertiku yang tidak peduli pada dunia atau kesenangan dan daya tarik materialnya. Atau akibat dari tindakanmu tidak akan baik."

Sang dermawan bagi umat manusia, Nabi ﷺ menjawab dengan nada yang lembut:

"Kamu berkata dusta. Kehormatan atau kehinaan di mata dunia sepenuhnya berada dalam kendali Allah ﷻ."

Setelah menjawab tantangan kurang ajar dan kasar dari orang tersebut dengan tenang dan lembut, beliau berjalan masuk ke masjid dan memimpin salat dengan penuh konsentrasi dan kekhusyukan. Setelah menunaikan salat kepada Penciptanya, Nabi ﷺ mengalihkan perhatiannya kepada para tawanan,
content_ar: They came out of the capital, Madyan, and got on to their camels with the basket of soil. On reaching the presence of Sa’d bin Abu Waqs +3, they gave him the الخير news of النصر and said: “الله Almighty will conquer for the المسلمون the land of Persia. And they will be masters of Iran.” A. few days later the المعركة of Qadissiyah was قاتل and the trenches of the المعركةfield of Qadissiyah were filled with dead bodies. But all these bodies were those of the Iranian soldiers. Though the Iranians faced a humiliating الهزيمة at Qadissiyah, they did not lose الشجاعة; من ناحية أخرى they conscripted new recruits and organised an الجيش numbering one hundred and fifty thousand soldiers. عندما the Caliph, ‘عمر Farooq %, heard of this huge الجيش, he himself got ready to face this great danger to the مسلم الأمة. But some المهم personages of the الأمة prevented him from doing لذا. They suggested that a القائد should be given the المسؤولية of leading the الجيش on this expedition, and it would be better إذا he continued to remain in the capital, Al-المدينة المنورة. ‘عمر Farooq # asked them to suggest the name of one particular person who would best be able to fulfil the المسؤولية of leading the مسلم الجيش. They all replied in chorus that he himself knew the الجيش very well. بعد considering the matter for a few minutes قال: “swear by الله Almighty that J will appoint such a person to lead the مسلم المجاهدين who at the time of action in the المعركةfield will appear swifter than an arrow; sucha person is Nu’man bin Magqran 2. ” As soon as they heard this they all exclaimed that in الحق he was the ideal person for the position of القائد. بعد gaining the vote of confidence of the members of the Advisory Council and the الجسد of Ministers of Administration and Management, ‘عمر Farooq -# wrote the following letter to Nu’man bin Maqran . “To Nu’man bin Magqran -# from ‘عمر bin Khattab (<4). Assalamu ‘Alikum twa Rahmathullah, my best wishes to you. I have received news that the Iranians have gathered a huge armed force in the city of Nahawind. بعد you receive my letter, please set out to oppose this Iranian الجيش with the Name of الله Almighty on your lips and with trust in His Assistance. Please remember that a single مسلم is more precious to me than a hundred thousand dinars. With Salam.” Nu’man bin Magqran .# prepared his الجيش for combat and sent experience warriors to get information about the prevailing situation. As these riders approached the city of Nahawind, their steeds suddenly stopped. The ace riders tried to spur them on but instead of moving forwards they started to rear up. عندما the riders got off to check on the problem they were surprised to find that nails had pierced their hooves. They immediately informed their القائد, Nu’man bin Maqran of the situation and asked for his opinion or advice on what to do next. He asked them to stay put as they were and عندما the darkness of night fell they should light huge bonfires to enable the العدو to spot them in the bright light produced by them. عندما the light of the fire spread far and wide, they should pretend to be scared and terrified and swiftly race backwards. The العدو should feel that they were terrified and were retreating. Then the العدو would experience an intense desire to capture them; من أجل accomplish this they would chase them and themselves get pierced by the nails and thorns they had spread out for the المجاهدين. وهكذا عندما these nails and thorns got embedded in the hooves of their steeds, the الطريق would be cleared for the مسلم soldiers. This ploy worked against the Iranians and عندما in the glow of the fire they saw that the مسلم soldiers wanted to retreat, they sent their workers to clear the roads. عندما the roads were cleared the المجاهدين suddenly turned around and advanced towards Nahawind and attacked the العدو. Nu’man bin Magran %, leading his men, reached the northern borders of Nahawind and addressing المجاهدين, said: “1 will raise my voice in praising الله Almighty and say ‘Allaho Akbar’. عندما I raise my voice the أولاً time, you should all become alert, عندما | call out the ثانياً time get your weapons ready. And عندما I raise my voice the ثالثاً time, all of you should attack the العدو with me.” لذا, Nu’man bin Maqran .% repeated the phrase three times and exhibiting great daring and الشجاعة, rushed into the ranks of the العدو. And under his leadership the المجاهدين, swept the العدو like pieces of straw on a forceful current and moved forward rapidly and relentlessly. A forceful المعركة ensued, مثل has been rarely paralleled in history. The Iranian soldiers were mowed down like a sheaves of grain, and one could only see heaps of bodies tying all over the المعركةfield. The blood of the sinners was flowing like water and the المعركةfield had become like a swamp. The horse of the القائد-inChief, Nu‘man bin Magran 4, had become uncontrollable and slipped in the slimy mud. As soon as Nu‘man bin Maqran -# fell from his horse he was الشهيدed. His الأخ stepped forward, picked up the standard, and threw his sheet over his الأخ's الجسد, لذا that the rest of the men did not realise their القائد-in-Chief had been الشهيدed. “Look, this is your General in the eternal sleep of الموت; having achieved الشهيدdom he has entered his name in letters of gold among the people in الجنة.” The eyes of all the loyal المجاهدين were filled with tears at the sight. “Come back to your Lord, well-pleased (yourself) and wellpleasing (unto Him)!” (89:28) Qa‘qa‘ bin ‘Amr At-Tameemi 2 On one occasion, النبي ﷺ 3, asked Qa‘qa‘ bin ‘Amr At-Tameemi #, “What preparations have you made for الجهاد?” رد on hearing this: “I have المحبة and obedience for النبي ﷺ 3B and a swift and daring horse. This is what I have made ready.” Qa‘qa‘ bin ‘Amr At-Tameemi Qa‘qa’ bin ‘Amr At-Tameemi < was a great General of the مسلم الجيش. He was a picture of disciplined obedience, a well built, handsome and attractive great hero of the مسلم الأمة who was extremely dignified. A brave, and daring soldier, an ace rider - Mujahid. Who was faster than wind, and an exemplary champion and supporter of الإسلام. He put to الخير use his experience as a warrior and could be depended upon at the most of البالغ الأهمية times. He did not get many opportunities to display his incomparable qualities of valour and military skills خلال the time of النبي ﷺ 38, لأن he joined the circle of the followers of الإسلام late. Nonetheless, in Qadissiyah, Nahawind and in each of the المعركةs against the apostates, he left lasting impressions of martial expertise and skill which are inscribed in history in golden letters. أبو بكر Siddiq 4 knew very well his incomparable الشجاعة, his lightening speed, his foresight and expertise in assaulting the العدو and paid him the compliment quoted above. بعد the المعركة of Tabook, in the year 9 Hijrah many tribes entered the fold of الإسلام. من أجل receive in person البركات from the benefactor of Mankind, النبي ﷺ محمد ﷺ 38, many crowds came to delight in his company and see him with their own eyes. very angry and addressing another trusted Companion, النبي 3 asked: “Who will go to accomplish this mission?” ‘Ainiah bin Badr said: “O النبي 38 may my الأم and my الأب be sacrificed for you, please give me a chance to serve you. | would المحبة to volunteer myself.” The benefactor of Mankind, النبي ﷺ 3%, appointed him an officer and placed fifty المجاهدين under him. He attacked the Banu Tameem, الهزيمةed them and captured eleven men and women and brought them back to Al-المدينة المنورة. Among the prisoners was the Chieftain, ‘Atarad. عندما these prisoners were brought in, النبي ﷺ % happened to be ina private room next to his المسجد. Bilal 4 had given the Adhan for the Dhuhr الصلاة and النبي ﷺ 3 was a little late in coming out to lead the الصلوات. The prisoners who were in chains outside النبي ﷺ’s المسجد became restless. One blunt outspoken prisoner, Iqr’a bin Habis, called out loudly from outside the room of النبي ﷺ #8: “Mister, release me from this imprisonment! You should be aware of the fact that I am a famous poet of my tribe. عندما I write الآيات praising anyone, the whole الدنيا looks at him with الحسد. And عندما I decide to become البالغ الأهمية of any person, then I sow such seeds of hatred and scorn that the man is not even able to lift up his head and walk upright among his peers. It is better for you O النبي 3% that you release a man like me who does not care for the الدنيا or its material pleasures and attractions. Or else the outcome of your actions will not be الخير.” The benefactor of Mankind, النبي ﷺ 38 replied in gentle tones: “You are speaking lie. الاحترام or disrespect in the eyes of the الدنيا is in the total control of الله 38.” بعد answering to the man’s insolent and rude challenge calmly and softly, he walked into the المسجد and led the الصلوات with full concentration and devotion.
